content.problemStatement:
Chef is an expert baker. To make a cake, he needs NNN different ingredients numbered 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. He needs AiA_iAi​ amount of the ingredient numbered iii, and he currently has BiB_iBi​ amount of that ingredient already.

Each ingredient costs him 111 coin to buy. Find the total cost of buying the excess ingredients necessary to make 111 cake.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the total number of ingredients.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the total cost of buying the necessary ingredients for 111 cake.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The cake has only one ingredient, of which you need 555 to make 111 cake. Since you already have 222 of that ingredient, you need to buy 333 more for a cost of 333.
Test Case 2 : The cake has 222 ingredients. You don't need anymore quantity of the ingredient 111, since you already have 222 of those but need only 111. However, you need 111 more quantity of the ingredient 222, since you have only 111 but need 222. Thus, you pay 111 coin to buy ingredient 222.
